timing_disclosure: Specified and revised twice prospectively during live Wave 3, after the completed valid post-Wave-2 audit reported four distinct strict canonical CodeContests problems. The initial rank-25 design failed closed because row 27 has 202 statically eligible candidates but only 24 JDK-valid accepted ranks. The first revision through rank 24 failed closed because row 54 has 255 statically eligible candidates but only 19 JDK-valid accepted ranks. No Wave-3 model output, result, score, completion, reasoning trace, or result file was inspected or used to specify or revise this contingency.
adaptive_status_disclosure: This second extension is adaptive and outcome-aware from the Wave-2 aggregate result, is not preregistered, and is not outcome-blind in timing. Its case eligibility, deterministic rank key, accepted-rank continuation, and both feasibility-driven prospective revisions are outcome-independent and use no model outcome. The final largest uniform two-wave design across the same 19 problems is Wave 4 ranks 11--15 and Wave 5 ranks 16--19; there is no Wave 6.
preparation_read_disclosure: Preparation, tokenizer gating, revisions, and freezing read no inference result directory or file, perform no language-model inference, and create no inference result root. The completed post-Wave-2 count of four is an externally supplied trigger and timing fact only; no Wave-3 output or result was inspected. Both preserved failures use static/JDK feasibility evidence only.

sequential_trigger.metric: distinct canonical CodeContests rows with at least one strict eligible sample
sequential_trigger.wave_4: launch only when the complete valid post-Wave-3 metric is below 10
sequential_trigger.wave_5: launch only after Wave 4 is complete and the complete valid post-Wave-4 metric is below 10

immutable_population.source_choice: the same first tokenizer-eligible variant per canonical row used by frozen balanced Waves 1--3

case_selection.public_tests_policy: not loaded, inspected, ranked, or selected
case_selection.output_line_definition: len(str.splitlines()); a terminal newline adds no line
case_selection.jdk_acceptance: both frozen source variants compile and run without timeout; exit zero and empty stderr; both outputs historical-trimmed-exact to the oracle and one another
case_selection.continuation_rule: reconstruct the deterministic rank stream, verify frozen accepted ranks 1--10 exactly, then retain ranks 11--15 for Wave 4 and ranks 16--19 for Wave 5; no Wave 6
case_selection.failure_policy: fail closed unless an exact 19-by-5 Wave 4 and exact 19-by-4 Wave 5 can be supplied; preserve both failed larger prospective designs

rank_20_feasibility_failure.proposal: revised three-wave extension through accepted rank 24

waves.trigger_audit: complete valid post-Wave-4 audit after Wave 4 completes
waves.revision_note: largest uniform second-wave block available across the same 19 problems; Wave 6 removed

requirements: prompt plus generation budget fits context

inference.codesteer_profile: registered long-code level-2 post-softmax slice_hybrid profile
inference.path_rule: all runner, manifest, and output-root arguments are HERE-relative to long-code-sample-work

strict_rule: complete valid matched 3x4 runs; at least one trimmed-exact CodeSteer marker-JSON trial; zero trimmed-exact trials for each of the three baselines

reporting.unique_problem_key: dataset id + revision + split + row index
